desc: Puno sits on Lake Titicaca's high-altitude shore, offering island-community excursions and Andean culture, with the driest clearest conditions usually concentrated in the austral winter months.
hls: Gateway for Lake Titicaca boat trips including Uros floating islands and Taquile/Amantaní communities.
High elevation (~3,800m) requires slower pacing, hydration, and first-day acclimatization.
Driest weather generally runs May-September; wet-season storms are more common Dec-March.
Strong destination for living-culture encounters, weaving traditions, and lake landscapes.
Pairs naturally with Cusco/Sacred Valley itineraries via rail or overland routes.
Large day-night temperature drops are normal, even in sunnier dry-season months.

todo.name: Uros floating islands visit
todo.desc: Take a short boat excursion to understand reed-island construction, daily life, and local handicrafts.

todo.name: Taquile or Amantaní day/overnight
todo.desc: Choose one major island for walking trails, textile traditions, and family-run meal experiences.

todo.name: Puno waterfront + market walk
todo.desc: Combine lakeside viewpoints with a local market loop for produce, snacks, and everyday city rhythm.

todo.name: Sillustani chullpas
todo.desc: Visit the pre-Inca funerary towers near Umayo Lagoon, ideally timed for softer late-day light.

todo.name: Folklore evening
todo.desc: Add a dance/music performance night to connect with regional costume and festival traditions.

todo.name: Acclimatization recovery block
todo.desc: Plan lighter activity after arrival or after long transit days to reduce altitude fatigue.

wifi.note: Good enough for core planning tasks in central hotels; speeds are weaker on islands and during peak evening usage.

prac.fltNote: No UK nonstop and no major airport in Puno itself; most routes combine UK flights to Lima/Cusco/Juliaca plus overland or domestic transfer.
prac.currency: Peruvian Sol (PEN). Carry cash for boat docks, smaller restaurants, and island purchases.
prac.lang: Spanish, Quechua, and Aymara are common; English availability is mixed outside organized tours.
prac.visa: Confirm Peru entry and onward transit requirements before travel, especially for multi-country Andean routes.
alerts: Altitude effects are common at this elevation; avoid over-scheduling on day one.
Wet-season storms can disrupt boat comfort and visibility.
Night temperatures can be near or below freezing in dry months.

sweet: May to September is usually the safest window for clearer lake conditions and lower rain disruption, with July-August being busiest.
